lessons.text: Cast off weakness of heart and rise to act.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's opening rebuke to shake off paralyzing despondency and stand up.

lessons.text: When your duty confuses you, seek a wise teacher.
lessons.rationale: Captures Arjuna surrendering as a disciple asking for instruction, distinct from the despair verses around it.

lessons.text: The wise grieve for neither the living nor the dead.
lessons.rationale: The opening thesis of the whole discourse: grief itself is the error.

lessons.text: The self endures unchanged through youth, age, and death.
lessons.rationale: Anchors to the specific image of passing through life's stages into another body.

lessons.text: Bear pleasure and pain evenly; both come and go.
lessons.rationale: Sense-contact brings impermanent heat and cold; the one who endures them is fit for immortality.

lessons.text: The unreal never is; the real never ceases to be.
lessons.rationale: A pure metaphysical distinction between the transient and the permanent, unlike the endurance lessons.

lessons.text: Bodies perish, but what pervades them cannot be destroyed.
lessons.rationale: The indestructible pervader versus the finite body; concludes 'therefore fight.'

lessons.text: The self is never the slayer nor the slain.
lessons.rationale: The unborn, unkillable self neither acts as killer nor is killed.

lessons.text: Death exchanges bodies like worn-out clothes for new.
lessons.rationale: The distinctive changing-garments image for reincarnation.

lessons.text: No weapon, fire, water, or wind can harm the soul.
lessons.rationale: The concrete four-elements image asserting the self's invulnerability.

lessons.text: The self is unmanifest and beyond the mind's reach.
lessons.rationale: The self as unthinkable and unchangeable, a reason not to grieve.

lessons.text: Even were death final, grief cannot undo the inevitable.
lessons.rationale: The alternative argument: even granting mortality, death is certain, so grief is pointless.

lessons.text: The self is a marvel that few ever truly grasp.
lessons.rationale: The wonder-and-mystery verse: seen, spoken of, heard, yet unknown.

lessons.text: The same deathless self dwells within every being.
lessons.rationale: The universality angle: the indestructible indweller in all, so grieve for none.

lessons.text: Do your rightful duty rather than shrink from it.
lessons.rationale: Duty framed as obligation and opportunity, with abandonment counted as sin.

lessons.text: Fleeing your duty brings dishonor worse than death.
lessons.rationale: The worldly reputation argument, distinct from the duty-as-sin appeal before it.

lessons.text: Meet victory and defeat, gain and loss, alike.
lessons.rationale: The pivot verse equalizing all outcomes before acting.

lessons.text: On this path no effort is ever wasted.
lessons.rationale: Introduces Yoga of action: no loss, and a little of it guards from great fear.

lessons.text: Hold one clear resolve; scattered aims lead nowhere.
lessons.rationale: Single-pointed determination against the many-branched thoughts of the irresolute.

lessons.text: Chasing pleasure and reward leaves the mind unsettled.
lessons.rationale: Those enchanted by flowery promises of enjoyment never reach steady concentration.

lessons.text: Rise above the pairs of opposites and possessiveness.
lessons.rationale: Transcend the three gunas, opposites, and the urge to acquire and hoard.

lessons.text: To the awakened, scripture is a well amid a flood.
lessons.rationale: The reservoir-in-a-flood image: direct knowing makes borrowed texts superfluous.

lessons.text: You have a right to your work, never its results.
lessons.rationale: The keystone teaching on action without claim to the fruit.

lessons.text: Even-mindedness in success and failure is true yoga.
lessons.rationale: Defines yoga specifically as evenness of mind while acting.

lessons.text: Ground action in wisdom; that is skill in action.
lessons.rationale: Wise action transcends good and evil, far above work driven by reward.

lessons.text: Release the fruits of action and be freed from bondage.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing fruits specifically frees one from the bonds of rebirth.

lessons.text: Cross beyond delusion and lose your taste for empty words.
lessons.rationale: Passing the mire of delusion brings indifference to what is heard and to be heard.

lessons.text: When your mind rests unmoved in the self, you awaken.
lessons.rationale: Steadiness of intellect in the self, not scripture, yields realization.

lessons.text: True contentment is found within, not in fulfilled desires.
lessons.rationale: First mark of steady wisdom: casting off desires and being satisfied in the self.

lessons.text: Stay unshaken by sorrow and free of craving and fear.
lessons.rationale: The sage undisturbed by adversity and released from attachment, fear, and anger.

lessons.text: Neither celebrate the good nor recoil from the bad.
lessons.rationale: Firm wisdom neither rejoices at fortune nor hurries from misfortune.

lessons.text: Withdraw your senses as a tortoise draws in its limbs.
lessons.rationale: The signature tortoise image for sense-withdrawal.

lessons.text: Abstaining curbs the act; only insight ends the craving.
lessons.rationale: Objects leave the abstinent but the underlying longing fades only on seeing the Supreme.

lessons.text: Even the wise can be swept away by restless senses.
lessons.rationale: A warning that turbulent senses overpower even one striving to control them.

lessons.text: Master your senses and fix your mind on the highest.
lessons.rationale: Restraint paired with devotion to the divine steadies wisdom.

lessons.text: Dwelling on objects breeds desire, then anger, then ruin.
lessons.rationale: The famous descent chain from brooding to attachment, anger, delusion, and destruction.

lessons.text: Move among temptations without craving or aversion to find peace.
lessons.rationale: The self-controlled act in the world free of attraction and repulsion, ending all pain.

lessons.text: Without a steady mind there is no peace or joy.
lessons.rationale: The negative chain: no wisdom, meditation, peace, or happiness for the unsteady.

lessons.text: Rein in the wandering senses that pull the mind off course.
lessons.rationale: The wind-and-boat image: one loose sense drags away discrimination, so restrain them all.

lessons.text: What the world chases, the wise quietly turn from.
lessons.rationale: The night-and-day inversion of the sage's values against the world's.

lessons.text: Let desires enter and pass like rivers into the sea.
lessons.rationale: The ocean image: peace comes to one whom desires enter without disturbing.

lessons.text: Drop craving, ownership, and ego to arrive at peace.
lessons.rationale: Peace through abandoning longing, the sense of 'mine,' and egoism.

lessons.text: Reach this state and even death cannot delude you.
lessons.rationale: The concluding Brahmic state, undeluded and attained even at life's end.

skipped.reason: narrative: Sanjaya describing the grieving Arjuna
narrative: Arjuna's objection to fighting his elders
narrative: Arjuna's despair over slaying teachers
narrative: Arjuna's uncertainty about the outcome
narrative: Arjuna's lament that no gain removes his sorrow
narrative: Sanjaya reporting Arjuna's refusal to fight
narrative: Arjuna's question setting up the steady-wisdom description
